claim.text: 12,000 SF × 0.375 = 4,500 SF max footprint
claim.context: Maximum Lot Coverage per Denver Zoning Code 37.5% (lots >30' wide): 12,000 SF × 0.375 = 4,500 SF max footprint

verdicts.note: The 37.5% maximum lot coverage for lots wider than 30 feet is confirmed in Denver Zoning Code, and the mathematical calculation (12,000 SF × 0.375 = 4,500 SF) is correct.

claim.text: Maximum lot coverage 37.5% for lots >30' wide
claim.context: Maximum Lot Coverage per Denver Zoning Code 37.5% (lots >30' wide)

verdicts.note: Multiple authoritative sources confirm Denver Zoning Code establishes 37.5% maximum lot coverage for lots 30 feet wide or greater, with 50% coverage for smaller lots.

claim.text: 45,000 SF ÷ 5 stories = 9,000 SF building footprint
claim.context: Building Footprint calculated as 45,000 SF ÷ 5 stories = 9,000 SF

verdicts.note: The calculation methodology is mathematically incorrect and contradicts standard building code definitions; building footprint is a horizontal measurement at grade level, not derived by dividing total square footage by number of stories.

claim.text: ADA spaces 2% minimum of total parking spaces
claim.context: ADA Spaces per IBC/ADA 2% minimum of total spaces

verdicts.note: The 2% requirement applies only to residential occupancies (R-2, R-3, R-4) and to parking lots with 501-1,000 spaces; it is not a universal minimum for all ADA parking spaces as the claim suggests.

claim.text: IBC/ADA parking space requirements
claim.context: ADA Spaces per IBC/ADA 2% minimum of total spaces

verdicts.note: The 2% minimum ADA parking space requirement is explicitly confirmed in Denver's adopted IBC (2018 and 2021 versions) for residential occupancies and is consistent with ADA standards for larger parking facilities.

claim.text: Within 10 feet of primary street: maximum 1-foot grade difference
claim.context: Within 10 feet of primary street: maximum 1-foot difference between original and finished grade

verdicts.note: The exact requirement is confirmed in Denver's official Zoning Tip Sheet 3 from the Community Planning and Development department, citing DZC Section 10.6.5.

claim.text: Retaining walls limited to 4 feet maximum height in primary street setback
claim.context: Retaining walls limited to 4 feet maximum height in primary street setback

verdicts.note: The claim is explicitly confirmed in multiple authoritative Denver sources, including official zoning tip sheets and recent Board of Adjustment decisions citing the 4-foot maximum height requirement for retaining walls in primary street setbacks.

claim.text: Stormwater discharge permit required for projects >1 acre
claim.context: Stormwater discharge permit required for projects >1 acre

verdicts.note: Denver's official Construction Activities Stormwater Manual explicitly requires a CASDP for projects disturbing one acre or more of land.

claim.text: Construction Activities Stormwater Discharge Permit (CASDP) required
claim.context: Construction Activities Stormwater Discharge Permit (CASDP) required

verdicts.note: The Construction Activities Stormwater Discharge Permit (CASDP) is explicitly confirmed as a required permit in Denver's official regulatory documents for construction activities disturbing one or more acres.

claim.text: Site Development Plan (SDP) required for mixed-use construction
claim.context: Site Development Plan (SDP) required for mixed-use construction

verdicts.note: Denver's official sources confirm that Site Development Plans are required for commercial construction and residential projects with three or more units, which are the components of mixed-use construction.

claim.text: EV charging infrastructure required per 2022 Denver Energy Code §R404.5
claim.context: EV charging infrastructure required per 2022 Denver Energy Code §R404.5

verdicts.note: Section R404.5 of the 2022 Denver Energy Code addresses "Additional Electric Infrastructure," not EV charging infrastructure; EV charging requirements are specified in R404.4.

claim.text: 2025 Denver Building Code
claim.context: Building Code compliance per 2025 Denver Building Code

verdicts.note: The 2025 Denver Building Code is confirmed as an official, currently adopted building code for Denver, with official documentation published on the City's authoritative domain.

claim.text: 2022 Denver Energy Code
claim.context: EV charging infrastructure required per 2022 Denver Energy Code §R404.5

verdicts.note: The 2022 Denver Energy Code exists, but §R404.5 governs additional electrical infrastructure, not EV charging infrastructure; EV charging requirements are in §R404.4 (residential) and §C405.13 (commercial).

claim.text: 2022 Denver Energy Code §R404.5
claim.context: EV charging infrastructure required per 2022 Denver Energy Code §R404.5

verdicts.note: While §R404.5 exists in the 2022 Denver Energy Code, it addresses electric infrastructure for fossil fuel appliances, not EV charging infrastructure; EV charging requirements are specified in §R404.4.

claim.text: Professional engineer certification required for drainage impacts
claim.context: Professional engineer certification required for drainage impacts

verdicts.note: Denver's authoritative sources explicitly require professional engineer certification and sealing for drainage-related work, including construction drawings, grading plans, and certificates of inspection.

claim.text: Proposed building footprint (9,000 SF) exceeds maximum lot coverage (4,500 SF)
claim.context: CRITICAL ISSUE: Proposed building footprint (9,000 SF) exceeds maximum lot coverage (4,500 SF). Design revision required.

verdicts.note: The specific claim with these exact figures (9,000 SF footprint exceeding 4,500 SF maximum) does not appear in searchable Denver government sources; Denver's actual lot coverage regulations use percentages (37.5%-50%) rather than fixed square footage limits.
